calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-21-2013 Continued To Feb-25-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-25-2013 Assigned To Trial Court

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Feb-25-2013 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from The Presiding Judge Department 206 for Entry of Judgment.
Department 514, Judge: Donald Sullivan, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.

M David DeSantis, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff Ming Zhu, LLC.
No appearance on behalf or by Defendants San Francisco Medical Imaging, Inc., and Ruvin Feygenberg.
Court ordered Defendants Does 1-10 dismissed without prejudice.
Michael Moretti, Property Manager sworn and examined on behalf of Plaintiff.

After evidence, Court ordered the following Judgment entered in favor of Plaintiff Ming Zhu, LLC and against Defendant San Francisco Medical Imaging, Inc., and Ruvin Feygenberg jointly and separetely as follows:
Unpaid rents $73,500.00
Repairs and removal of debris $5,972.00
Attorney's Fees and costs $26,018.98
Total Judgment in the amount of $105,490.98
and Judgment in favor of Plaintiff Ming Zhu, LLC and against Defendant Ruvin Feygenber as follows:
Unpaid rents $73,500.00
Repairs and removal of debris $5,972.00
Attorney's Fess and Costs $26,018.98
Judgment in Case CUD-10-633774 $53.662.00 plus Interest accumulated at the legal rate from entry of Judgment in the total amount of $64,394.40.
Total Judgment of $169,885.38
Counsel for Plaintiff to prepare Judgment for the court's signature.

Plaintiff Ming Zhu'S Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted.
Plaintiff Ming Zhu'S Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions is Granted in full, including $885.00 in sanctions, for reasons set forth in moving party's papers; Defendants Ruvin Feygenberg and San Francisco Medical Imaging, Inc. do not oppose the motion and have not provided any response to the requests for admission. Prevailing party to prepare order. Pro Tem Judge: Katherine Huibonhoa, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported.=302/jpt

Ntc Of Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:

PLAINTIFF MING ZHU, LLC'S Notice Of Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions is continued to December 20, 2012 to give Ming Zhu, LLC the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Ming Zhu, LLC must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date no later than December 13, 2012 at 4:00 pm, or the motion will be denied. Rebecca Woodson, Judge Pro-Tem, Jacqueline Alameda, Clerk, Not Reported. =302/JPT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-29-2012 Continued To Aug-15-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Amended Hearing Date On Amended Mtn And Amended Mtn To Set Aside The Court'S Order Of Mar 27, 2012; Dec And Pos
Off Calendar Jun-28-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Stella A. Havkin, Esq., of Litwak & Havkin, Tel. 818.999.1568, appearing for Defendant and moving party by court call.

No other appearance. The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FLORA IRA'S Amended Motion To Set Aside The Court's Order Of March 27, 2012 is Granted. No opposition filed. Defendant's counsel is ordered to submit a proposed order by email today. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-11-2012 Continued To Jul-11-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

Mtn To Set Aside Court'S Order Of 3/27/12
Off Calendar May-15-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Flora Ira, Tel. 818.442.8822, Defendant appearing on her own behalf via court call.
No other appearance.

The matter was discussed. DEFENDANT FLORA IRA Motion To Set Aside Court'S Order Of 3/27/12 is Denied without prejudice to being re-filed. The moving party did not provide courtesy copies of the motion to Department 302 as required by Local Rule 2.6B. Moreover, the court's records do not show that any order was issued or filed on March 27, 2012 and it appears that the motion may need to be heard in Department 610, not Department 302. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
